failure_surface: SyntaxError exceptions during code execution due to malformed code cells containing markdown/plain text instead of valid Python
root_cause: Agent generated code blocks that contained markdown formatting (bold text, bullet points, headers) or plain text responses instead of executable Python code, causing repeated SyntaxErrors that prevented any actual workbook manipulation from occurring
minimal_fix_hypothesis: Ensure all code execution blocks contain only syntactically valid Python statements with no markdown syntax, headers, or explanatory text interleaved within the code structure
evidence: Multiple SyntaxError messages showing invalid syntax at lines containing markdown (**text**, ### headers)
Code cell with '<tool_call>' character at line 1 causing immediate syntax error
Final execution claim reported success but test_case_results shows 0 failures (complete task failure)
OpenPyXL code appeared in one attempt but was surrounded by non-executable content in other attempts

memory_items.title: Code Cell Hygiene
memory_items.lesson: Always ensure code execution blocks contain only valid Python syntax without markdown formatting or plain text mixed in
memory_items.generalization: When generating code for execution, validate that the entire content block contains executable statements only; do not embed explanatory text, markdown headers, or formatted strings within code cells

memory_items.title: Markdown Separation
memory_items.lesson: Keep explanatory output and documentation separate from executable code to avoid syntax violations
memory_items.generalization: Use distinct communication channels for explanation vs. execution; never place task summary text, status reports, or formatted descriptions inside code execution parameters

memory_items.title: Pre-Execution Validation
memory_items.lesson: Before submitting code for execution, mentally verify the first and last lines contain valid Python syntax
memory_items.generalization: Implement a mental check for common code generation pitfalls: leading/trailing whitespace, special characters, markdown symbols, or non-Python language constructs that would cause immediate parsing failures

edits.content:
### 7. Code Generation Hygiene
- **Executable Integrity:** Code cells passed to Python interpreters must contain only valid Python syntax
- **No Mixed Content:** Never embed markdown (headers, bold, bullets), plain text, or explanations inside code execution blocks
- **Validation Check:** Verify first and last lines are valid Python statements before submission
- **Separation of Concerns:** Use separate communication channels for task explanations versus executable code
